title: Neillia incisa
taxonomy: Neillia incisa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a part of the genus Neillia, which consists of various species of shrubs and small trees. The genus Neillia is further grouped under the family Rosaceae, which includes other flowering plants like roses and apples. The classification of Neillia incisa is as follows: kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Rosales, family Rosaceae, genus Neillia, and species N. incisa. The specific classification of Neillia incisa may vary slightly depending on the source or taxonomic system used.
uses: Neillia incisa is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's stems and roots have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro: Neillia incisa, also known as Cut-leaved Neillia, is a deciduous shrub belonging to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Neillia incisa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neillia incisa is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in the production of herbal supplements and tonics.
